summary:
Orbital non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma predominantly involves B cells; thus, this subtype represents the most frequent occurrence among ocular cases. Answer provided was A based on these findings and typical patterns observed clinically for such malignancies at extra-nodal sites including orbits. ```plaintext
Orbital non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma primarily affects B cells, making them the most common type encountered clinically. Thus, option A correctly identifies B-cell lymphoma as the prevalent form in the orbit.
```
